title: is it eh va or ay va
display_title: is it eh va or ay va
wikitext:
[]: is it eh-va or ay-va?             Quote Post by captainhaddock » Sat Oct 21, 2006 4:45 pm It seems to me in the show they said it like ay-va, and in death and rebirth they kept saying eh-va.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 21, 2006 5:42 pm The pronunciation of "Evangelion" is incorrect in D&R and EoE because of Amanda Winn Lee not knowing a flip about anime, Japan, or Evangelion. Aside from the original Japanese, you might find these useful: http://www.evamonkey.com/audio/f0.mp3 http://www.evamonkey.com/audio/f1.mp3 http://www.evamonkey.com/audio/title_call_a.mp3 http://www.evamonkey.com/audio/title_call_b.mp3

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 21, 2006 10:50 pm Ain't you got an aunt Eva? You should get one, it helps.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sun Oct 22, 2006 3:26 pm it's original greek origin is like in what the Evamonkey gives, any other is wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Sun Oct 22, 2006 11:28 pm It took me months to learn how to pronunce "Manga"...so when I first found out abotu Eva, I knew my tongue was in for a rough ride. (Being a southern boy has it's faults!) I prounce Eva as "Eh-va" as they do in the English dub.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Oct 22, 2006 11:45 pm I've taken to "A-bwa"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 23, 2006 12:10 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: I prounce Eva as "Eh-va" as they do in the English dub.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Oct 23, 2006 12:12 am You say "eh-va", they say "ay-va". I say "ai ya". How did AWL get into this line of work, anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Oct 23, 2006 3:21 am isn't the "e" sound in Japanese close to a long "a" like in "net"? As in "Kimi Ga Nozomu Eien" or Hime or uhh, Eureka? IIRC, there's only 5 or so vowel sounds in Japanese and none of them presicely resemble a long "a", like in "game" (the "ay-va"). But it isn't as flat as a flat "e" as in "eh-va" that some of the VAs in the dubs use. I could be wrong, as I'm nowhere near an authority of the language, but since it is a proper noun in the Japanese language, shouldn't it be pronounced like any other proper noun originating from another language?

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Oct 23, 2006 6:47 am I pronounce it "Ay-Va" with a "Va-va-va!" Ah... Dr. Vink... Good memories. I'm going to blame everything on regional dialects in languages.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Oct 23, 2006 3:42 pm This entire matter is further complicated by that very American problem; the nasal 'a'.

[]:             Quote Post by captainhaddock » Mon Oct 23, 2006 3:59 pm thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 24, 2006 1:12 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: This entire matter is further complicated by that very American problem; the nasal 'a'.

[]:             Quote Post by darth_mark » Tue Oct 24, 2006 3:20 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: I knew my tongue was in for a rough ride.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 24, 2006 3:21 am darth_mark wrote: lol that reminds me of the time when I hitchhiked from coast to coast.

[]:             Quote Post by darth_mark » Tue Oct 24, 2006 3:25 am Reichu wrote: darth_mark wrote: lol that reminds me of the time when I hitchhiked from coast to coast.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Tue Oct 24, 2006 4:13 am Forrest Gump ran coast to coast >.> And the nasal "a" does appear in different situations. Of course, just blame it on the evolution of the Americanized English language.

[]:             Quote Post by toReason » Tue Oct 24, 2006 5:39 am I always say it as "eee vah"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 24, 2006 7:56 am Eternal Yamcha wrote: And the nasal "a" does appear in different situations. Of course, just blame it on the evolution of the Americanized English language.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Tue Oct 24, 2006 11:16 pm You know, we have a street in my neighborhood called "Evangline" and we use the eh-va prounciation. It's the last part of "Evangelion" that I'm kinda wondering about-- Do you say it like "Jellion" or "Gellion" (Hard J sound or hard G sound)?
